mechanism: complex system failure under test conditions: test depth compressed probable flooding, power, emergency blow, and rescue limits into one narrowing recovery envelope
primary_lane: Design Failures That Changed the World
revision_summary: Strengthened changed-first framing around test depth changing the recovery envelope.
Separated source-supported evidence from interpretation to test during script research.
Expanded compact 10-part spine for a 12-18 minute episode.
Added title directions, thumbnail direction, research needs, and review questions.
Preserved caution around inconclusive initiating cause, probable/postulated flooding, partial final communications, and avoiding individual blame.
guardrails: Do not state a single initiating cause as proven.
Treat brazed-joint failure, flooding, and the exact casualty chain as probable or postulated unless a primary source supports stronger language.
Present final communications as partial or garbled evidence, not a clean transcript scene.
Avoid individual-blame framing; center design, fabrication, QA, procedure, testing, and rescue-limit coupling.
review_questions: Is the changed-first frame specific enough: test depth changed the recovery envelope before the system fully recognized the casualty?
Does the packet preserve enough uncertainty around initiating cause while still giving the episode a strong mechanism?
Should SUBSAFE be introduced early as a promise of consequence, or reserved for the final third as the institutional answer?
Which title direction best balances familiarity and mechanism without implying a solved mystery?
blockers: Needs human/Mike writer-packet disposition before script drafting.
Initiating cause remains inconclusive; script stage must keep probable flooding and electrical/propulsion chain as postulated unless primary-source review supports stronger language.
